heading: Comprehensive opioid recovery centers
label: 290ee–7 Comprehensive opioid recovery centers

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants on a competitive basis to eligible entities to establish or operate a comprehensive opioid recovery center (referred to in this section as a “Center”). A Center may be a single entity or an integrated delivery network.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A grant awarded under subsection (a) shall be for a period of not less than 3 years and not more than 5 years.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: 2 Renewal
children.text: A grant awarded under subsection (a) may be renewed, on a competitive basis, for additional periods of time, as determined by the Secretary. In determining whether to renew a grant under this paragraph, the Secretary shall consider the data submitted under subsection (h).

children.heading: Minimum number of Centers
children.label: c Minimum number of Centers
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate the amounts made available under subsection (j) such that not fewer than 10 grants may be awarded. Not more than one grant shall be made to entities in a single State for any one period.

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 1 Eligible entity
children.text: An entity is eligible for a grant under this section if the entity offers treatment and other services for individuals with a substance use disorder.

children.label: A
children.text: evidence that such entity carries out, or has the demonstrated capability to carry out, through referral or contractual arrangements, the activities described in subsection (g); and

children.label: B
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

located in a State with an age-adjusted rate of drug overdose deaths that is above the national overdose mortality rate, as determined by the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention; or

serving an Indian Tribe (as defined in ) with an age-adjusted rate of drug overdose deaths that is above the national overdose mortality rate, as determined through appropriate mechanisms determined by the Secretary in consultation with Indian Tribes.section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Preference
children.label: f Preference
children.text: In awarding grants under subsection (a), the Secretary may give preference to eligible entities utilizing technology-enabled collaborative learning and capacity building models, including such models as defined in section 2 of the Expanding Capacity for Health Outcomes Act (; ), to conduct the activities described in this section.Public Law 114–270130 Stat. 1395

children.label: A
children.text: Ensure that intake, evaluations, and periodic patient assessments meet the individualized clinical needs of patients, including by reviewing patient placement in treatment settings to support meaningful recovery.

children.label: i
children.text: all drugs and devices approved or cleared under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act and all biological products licensed under to treat substance use disorders or reverse overdoses, pursuant to Federal and State law;section 262 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: medically supervised withdrawal management, that includes patient evaluation, stabilization, and readiness for and entry into treatment;

children.label: iii
children.text: counseling provided by a program counselor or other certified professional who is licensed and qualified by education, training, or experience to assess the psychological and sociological background of patients, to contribute to the appropriate treatment plan for the patient, and to monitor patient progress;

children.label: iv
children.text: treatment, as appropriate, for patients with co-occurring substance use and mental disorders;

children.label: v
children.text: testing, as appropriate, for infections commonly associated with illicit drug use;

children.label: vi
children.text: residential rehabilitation, and outpatient and intensive outpatient programs;

children.label: vii
children.text: recovery housing;

children.label: viii
children.text: community-based and peer recovery support services;

children.label: ix
children.text: job training, job placement assistance, and continuing education assistance to support reintegration into the workforce; and

children.label: x
children.text: other best practices to provide the full continuum of treatment and services, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: C
children.text: Ensure that all programs covered by the Center include medication-assisted treatment, as appropriate, and do not exclude individuals receiving medication-assisted treatment from any service.

children.label: D
children.text: Periodically conduct patient assessments to support sustained and clinically significant recovery, as defined by the Assistant Secretary for Mental Health and Substance Use.

children.label: E
children.text: Provide onsite access to medication, as appropriate, and toxicology services; for purposes of carrying out this section.

children.label: F
children.text: Operate a secure, confidential, and interoperable electronic health information system.

children.label: G
children.text: Offer family support services such as child care, family counseling, and parenting interventions to help stabilize families impacted by substance use disorder, as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: training and supervising outreach staff, as appropriate, to work with State and local health departments, health care providers, the Indian Health Service, State and local educational agencies, schools funded by the Indian Bureau of Education, institutions of higher education, State and local workforce development boards, State and local community action agencies, public safety officials, first responders, Indian Tribes, child welfare agencies, as appropriate, and other community partners and the public, including patients, to identify and respond to community needs;

children.label: B
children.text: ensuring that the entities described in subparagraph (A) are aware of the services of the Center; and

children.label: C
children.text: disseminating and making publicly available, including through the internet, evidence-based resources that educate professionals and the public on opioid use disorder and other substance use disorders, including co-occurring substance use and mental disorders.

children.heading: Data reporting and program oversight
children.label: h Data reporting and program oversight
A
children.text: the programs and activities funded by the grant;

children.label: B
children.text: health outcomes of the population of individuals with a substance use disorder who received services from the Center, evaluated by an independent program evaluator through the use of outcomes measures, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: C
children.text: the retention rate of program participants; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other information that the Secretary may require for the purpose of—ensuring that the Center is complying with all the requirements of the grant, including providing the full continuum of services described in subsection (g)(1)(B).1So in original.1

children.heading: Additional reporting for certain eligible entities
children.label: 2 Additional reporting for certain eligible entities
children.text: An entity carrying out activities described in subsection (g) through referral or contractual arrangements shall include in the submissions required under paragraph (1) information related to the status of such referrals or contractual arrangements, including an assessment of whether such referrals or contractual arrangements are supporting the ability of such entity to carry out such activities.

children.heading: Privacy
children.label: i Privacy
children.text: The provisions of this section, including with respect to data reporting and program oversight, shall be subject to all applicable Federal and State privacy laws.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: j Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2030 for purposes of carrying out this section.
